strategy_candidates.title: The 'Why' Behind the Flow
strategy_candidates.short_description: Explain the significance of balancing guided and unguided authoring for the user experience.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: Connecting the technical procedure to the human experience of journal writing can help re-establish interest in the methodology.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Explain why the researchers chose to include both 'guided' and 'unguided' authoring capabilities. Focus on the trade-off between user control and AI-driven inspiration in the context of personal journaling.
expected_answer_shape: A brief explanation of the design philosophy
The benefit of user-guided vs. unguided modes

strategy_candidates.title: DiaryMate vs. Standard LLMs
strategy_candidates.short_description: Compare this specific writing flow to common AI tools like ChatGPT.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: Relating new research to familiar technology can make the 'method' section feel more relevant and less abstract.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Contrast the 'guided authoring' approach mentioned here (using keywords and intent) with the typical 'prompt-and-response' interaction of standard LLM chatbots.
expected_answer_shape: Highlighting what makes DiaryMate's flow unique

strategy_candidates.title: The Big Picture
strategy_candidates.short_description: A high-level summary of the DiaryMate writing flow.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: If the user is feeling disengaged, a single, clear takeaway allows them to grasp the core concept without wading through the dense procedural details immediately.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Provide a one-sentence summary of how DiaryMate transitions from design goals to the actual writing procedure described in this section.
expected_answer_shape: A single, punchy sentence summarizing the system's core interaction logic
